name: Features Icon Trio

iconName.description: Lucide icon name in lowercase kebab-case (e.g. 'zap', 'shield-check', 'trending-up'). Resolved internally to a react-icons/lu component.

stat.description: Optional numeric punch-line above the title — e.g. '47s', '1.7x', '62%'. Renders mono.

statLabel.description: Optional one-line caption clarifying the stat.

bullets.description: Optional 2 to 4 supporting bullets rendered as a flat divider list under the description.

description: A flat icon-led features grid with 3 to 6 cells, soft hover lift, hairline dividers between cells, and optional editorial scaffolding around the grid. Each cell can carry a mono stat punch-line plus a 2-to-4 item bullet list under the description for evidence and sub-claims; around the grid an optional 4-up meta strip, a pull-quote, an outcome metrics band, a methodology footnote and a closing CTA let the section carry the depth of a long-form benefits page rather than a thin three-up row. The workhorse features section for SaaS, professional services, and product landing pages where info density matters more than visual flourish.

descriptions.descriptive: Each cell stacks an icon chip, an optional mono-numeral stat with caption, a title, a description, and an optional flat bullet list using border-t divide-y rhythm — never card-in-card. Three items render as a 3-column row; four or more wrap to a 2-column or 2x3 grid to avoid the recognizable 4-or-6-across pattern. Cells share faded vertical hairline dividers, a 1px outline that activates on hover, and a 4-to-5 pixel hover lift. Above the grid an optional 4-up meta strip (mono-caps labels) frames the section as a single engagement; below it an optional serif pull-quote with primary border-l, a dark rounded outcome-metrics band with mono numerals, a mono methodology footnote and a closing CTA close the case. Every micro-label uses font-mono with 0.20-0.22em tracking. Reduced-motion users get a static fallback; everyone else gets a 0.06s staggered fade-up reveal.
descriptions.usage: Use as a benefits or capabilities section on SaaS landing pages, professional services sites, and product overview pages. Best when the brand needs a flat, scannable layout rather than the split layouts already in the library. Lean on the optional stat / bullets / meta / pullQuote / metrics / footnote slots when each feature needs evidence, not just a tagline — the section can then carry a full benefits page on its own. Pairs naturally below a hero and above pricing or testimonials.
descriptions.audienceFit: Suits info-dense brands like B2B SaaS, fintech, legal and consulting practices, and eco or sustainability brands that want a calm, credible features section without bento or asymmetric layouts. Works in both light and dark themes through semantic tokens.
